content.text: I'll help you review all the scheduled cronjobs on your Mittwald project. Let me start by listing the projects and then finding the cronjobs.

input.prompt:
You have access to Mittwald MCP tools. Your task is to:
1. First, list all projects using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list to see what projects are available
2. Then, for the main project (likely p-b3a3k3 or another accessible project), list all cronjobs using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_cronjob_list
3. For each cronjob found, get its execution history using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_cronjob_get_execution_history
4. Return a comprehensive summary showing:
   - All cronjobs found with their details (name, schedule, app, status, etc.)
   - Execution history for each cronjob (last runs, success/failure status, timestamps)
   - Any relevant error messages or details from recent executions

Present the information in a clear, organized format that helps the user understand their cronjob configuration and recent activity.

content.text:

3. For each cronjob found, get its execution history using mcp__mittwald__mittwald_cronjob_get_execution_history
4. Return a comprehensive summary showing:
   - All cronjobs found with their details (name, schedule, app, status, etc.)
   - Execution history for each cronjob (last runs, success/failure status, timestamps)

Present the information in a clear, organized format that helps the user understand their cronjob configuration and recent activity.

content.content:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

tool_use_result.stdout:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

content.content:
List cron jobs belonging to a project.

USAGE
  $ mw cronjob list -o txt|json|yaml|csv|tsv [--token <value>] [-x]
    [--no-header] [--no-truncate] [--no-relative-dates] [--csv-separator ,|;]
    [-p <value>]

FLAGS
  -o, --output=<option>         (required) [default: txt] output in a more
                                machine friendly format
                                <options: txt|json|yaml|csv|tsv>
  -p, --project-id=<value>      ID or short ID of a project; this flag is
                                optional if a default project is set in the
                                context
  -x, --extended                show extended information
      --csv-separator=<option>  [default: ,] separator for CSV output (only
                                relevant for CSV output)
                                <options: ,|;>
      --no-header               hide table header
      --no-relative-dates       show dates in absolute format, not relative
                                (only relevant for txt output)
      --no-truncate             do not truncate output (only relevant for txt
                                output)

AUTHENTICATION FLAGS
  --token=<value>  API token to use for authentication (overrides environment
                   and config file). NOTE: watch out that tokens passed via this
                   flag might be logged in your shell history.

DESCRIPTION
  List cron jobs belonging to a project.

ALIASES
  $ mw project cronjob list

FLAG DESCRIPTIONS
  -p, --project-id=<value>

    ID or short ID of a project; this flag is optional if a default project is
    set in the context

    May contain a short ID or a full ID of a project; you can also use the "mw
    context set --project-id=<VALUE>" command to persistently set a default
    project for all commands that accept this flag.

tool_use_result.stdout:
List cron jobs belonging to a project.

USAGE
  $ mw cronjob list -o txt|json|yaml|csv|tsv [--token <value>] [-x]
    [--no-header] [--no-truncate] [--no-relative-dates] [--csv-separator ,|;]
    [-p <value>]

FLAGS
  -o, --output=<option>         (required) [default: txt] output in a more
                                machine friendly format
                                <options: txt|json|yaml|csv|tsv>
  -p, --project-id=<value>      ID or short ID of a project; this flag is
                                optional if a default project is set in the
                                context
  -x, --extended                show extended information
      --csv-separator=<option>  [default: ,] separator for CSV output (only
                                relevant for CSV output)
                                <options: ,|;>
      --no-header               hide table header
      --no-relative-dates       show dates in absolute format, not relative
                                (only relevant for txt output)
      --no-truncate             do not truncate output (only relevant for txt
                                output)

AUTHENTICATION FLAGS
  --token=<value>  API token to use for authentication (overrides environment
                   and config file). NOTE: watch out that tokens passed via this
                   flag might be logged in your shell history.

DESCRIPTION
  List cron jobs belonging to a project.

ALIASES
  $ mw project cronjob list

FLAG DESCRIPTIONS
  -p, --project-id=<value>

    ID or short ID of a project; this flag is optional if a default project is
    set in the context

    May contain a short ID or a full ID of a project; you can also use the "mw
    context set --project-id=<VALUE>" command to persistently set a default
    project for all commands that accept this flag.

content.content:
Manage cronjobs of your projects

USAGE
  $ mw cronjob COMMAND

TOPICS
  cronjob execution  Manage individual cronjob executions

COMMANDS
  cronjob create   Create a new cron job
  cronjob delete   Delete a cron job
  cronjob execute  Manually run a cron job
  cronjob get      Get details of a cron job
  cronjob list     List cron jobs belonging to a project.
  cronjob update   Update an existing cron job

content.content:
Manage individual cronjob executions

USAGE
  $ mw cronjob execution COMMAND

COMMANDS
  cronjob execution abort  Abort a running cron job execution.
  cronjob execution get    Get a cron job execution.
  cronjob execution list   List CronjobExecutions belonging to a Cronjob.
  cronjob execution logs   Get the log output of a cronjob execution.

If you need to create cronjobs for any of your applications, you can use the Mittwald CLI:

```bash
# Create a cronjob
mw cronjob create --project-id <PROJECT_ID> \
  --description "My cronjob" \
  --interval "*/5 * * * *" \
  --command "/path/to/script.php"

# List cronjobs (after creation)
```
